plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim SMH and CRWD to reduce concentration.
Buy NVDA as a high-quality tech addition.
Reduce SPY to meet concentration limits.
Increase QQQI to boost income generation.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a larger cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan targets ~1.5% monthly growth with a focus on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash position for stability. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce overall leverage.
Reduce SMH exposure and increase CRWD for quality tech focus.
Add NVDA as a strong, high-scoring tech name.
Trim SPY to meet concentration limits.
This balanced approach blends growth with risk reduction.
balanced.read: This plan targets ~2.5% monthly growth with a risk-first approach. It trims leverage and reduces concentration while adding high-quality tech names. Drawdowns are managed but will be larger than the conservative plan.

plan: Increase SOXL and add SPAL to leverage strong semiconductor trends.
Add to SMH and CRWD to concentrate on high-conviction names.
Buy NVDA as a top-tier tech opportunity.
Trim SPY to allow for concentration in growth areas.
This aggressive plan seeks higher returns through leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This plan targets ~4% monthly growth by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring tech and semiconductor names. It carries significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and the impact of leveraged ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and SMH to remove volatility.
Trim CRWD and SPY to reduce concentration and high-beta exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI for steady income generation.
Add XLF to diversify and capture financial sector income.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly income over growth.
income.read: This plan targets ~1% monthly growth, focusing on generating steady income through QQQI and XLF. It minimizes volatility by exiting leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names, holding a larger cash buffer. NAV can still decline in market downturns.
